The Instagram
Passive Sales System
Your complete field guide for turning your Instagram profile into a system that converts cold traffic into buyers — on autopilot.
Beta Student Dashboard
The Big Idea 30-Day Roadmap Module 1 Module 2 Module 3 Module 4 Launch Sequence ✦ Hook Bank ✦
Start Here
The Big Idea
Everything else in this system flows from one foundational shift. Read this first.
The Reframe
Stop treating Instagram like a content hamster wheel. Start using it like a sales page.

Most people show up to Instagram with a posting schedule, a content calendar, and the vague hope that consistency will eventually turn into income. They're on the hamster wheel — creating constantly, burning out fast, and wondering why the followers aren't converting.

This system is the opposite. Instead of asking "what should I post today?" you ask "is my profile built to convert?" Build it highly curated and extremely strategic — every element with a job — and then simply drive traffic to it. The profile does the selling. You just have to build it once and keep the ads running.

The Shift in One Sentence
Instagram isn't a content platform you perform on every day. It's a sales page you build once, then send traffic to.

A great sales page has a clear headline, trust builders, social proof, a call to action, and supporting copy. Your Instagram has all of those things — a bio, highlights, pinned posts, and a feed. The only question is whether they're doing their job.

The goal is never followers. The goal is converting cold traffic into buyers. Every decision you make about your profile should run through that filter.

The Curation Rule
You are not building an audience. You are building a sales page. Post with intention, not consistency. Hide what doesn't convert — your feed is a living document, not a diary.
The Full Conversion Path
When cold ad traffic lands on your profile, here's the journey you've built:
📣
Ad stops the scroll
Your hook in the first frame is doing the heavy lifting here. This is why the post type system matters.
📸
Profile photo — "she looks like me"
Warm and approachable wins. Real over polished. Trust starts with the face before a word is read.
✍️
Bio — "this is for me, she has proof, I know what to do"
Four lines. Four jobs. No decorative copy.
🗂️
Highlights — "okay, she's not a scammer, other people have done this"
Handles the sales conversation passively, before anyone DMs.
📌
Pinned posts — "I believe this is possible, I want to know how"
Each pin handles a different part of the buyer's internal conversation.
📱
Feed — "she keeps showing up with this, I need to DM her"
Reinforce trust, handle objections, drive funnel entry.
🤖
ManyChat funnel → sale
The comment keyword triggers the automation. The sale happens inside the DMs.
Rebecca's Profile — Live Example
Here's the system working in the real world

Every layer you're about to learn is visible right here. Nothing is accidental.

📸
Profile photo: Warm, approachable, real. Not over-produced. Signals "I'm like you" before a single word is read.
✍️
Bio (4 lines, 4 jobs): Line 1 = value prop. Line 2 = "Mom of 3" identity qualifier. Line 3 = $2M proof. Line 4 = one clear CTA with the link.
🗂️
Highlights in order: "NOT a guru" kills the #1 objection first. Then Q&A, then student wins, then freebies. Skepticism gets neutralized before proof.
📌
3 pinned posts: Relatable Proof ("I'm just a regular mom of 3…") → Action Trigger ("Here's EXACTLY what I'd do…") → Emotional Proof ("My Online Business Changed Our Lives").
📊
1.9M views / 30 days from only 18 posts. More followers ≠ more sales. A high-converting profile + paid traffic = sales.
Beta Cohort — 30-Day Plan
Your Roadmap to Results
This is a 30-day sprint. The goal: fully implemented and seeing real results before Rebecca launches publicly in early May — with your story as a case study.
Week 1
Foundation
✓Read this entire dashboard
✓Optimize bio — 4 lines, 4 jobs
✓Build or update your 4 highlights
✓Create 3 pinned posts
✓Set up your freebie + email funnel
Week 2
Feed + ManyChat
✓Build out 12 feed posts (3 Objection Crushers, 3 Proof, 3 Curiosity Gap, 3 Qualifiers)
✓Curate live feed to match the ratio
✓Set up ManyChat with the template
✓Test your ManyChat flow end-to-end
✓Share your profile — get feedback
Week 3
Turn On Traffic
✓Launch traffic ad ($5–10/day)
✓Launch engagement ad ($5–10/day)
✓Note your starting metrics (views, DMs, sales)
✓Watch ManyChat DM volume daily
✓Note first sales or funnel entries
Week 4
Optimize + Report
✓Keep both ads running
✓Scale budget on the best performer if results are there
✓Screenshot wins + DM conversations
✓Document your results for the case study
✓Share your story in the Slack channel
🎯
The Goal
Fully implemented by end of Week 4. Real sales or funnel entries documented. Rebecca films the course content throughout April using your implementation as proof. Early May launch uses your results as the case study. You are the testimonial.
Before You Build
The 4 Things That Make This Work
The Instagram profile system is the engine. These four components are the fuel. All four need to be in place for the machine to run.
📱
Component 1
A Curated Instagram Profile
Your profile has to be built to Rebecca's framework before you send any traffic to it. Bio, highlights, pinned posts, and feed — all four layers doing their jobs. This is what the entire course teaches. Do not run ads to an unoptimized profile.
Your checkpoint: Walk through your profile as a cold stranger. Does every layer hand them to the next?
🎁
Component 2
A Freebie + Email Funnel
ManyChat collects their email and delivers the freebie. That email goes into a sequence that pitches your low-ticket offer — ideally with an order bump and upsell. The freebie warms them up. The email sequence closes the sale. This is your passive revenue engine running in the background.
Your checkpoint: Go through your own funnel as a buyer. Does every step feel like a natural next yes?
🤖
Component 3
ManyChat Set Up Correctly
ManyChat has to do two things: collect their email and deliver the freebie, AND pitch your offer immediately in the DM sequence. Most people only do the first. The immediate pitch in the DM — before the email sequence even starts — is where a significant percentage of sales come from. Use Rebecca's ManyChat template to set this up.
Your checkpoint: Comment your own keyword from a second account. Does the DM sequence pitch your offer before you even open your email?
📣
Component 4
2 Ad Types Running
A traffic ad drives cold strangers to your optimized profile. An engagement ad pushes your best posts in front of more people — generating comments, triggering ManyChat, and signaling to the algorithm that your content is worth amplifying. Both can run on $5–10/day each. Rebecca will walk you through setting both of these up — so don't stress about this one yet. The full setup is covered in Module 4.
Your checkpoint: Are both ads live? Check cost-per-DM after 7 days and scale the winner.
Module 1 of 4
The Mindset Shift
01
Module 1 — 3 Lessons
Why Your Instagram Isn't Converting (And How to Fix It)
Reframe how you think about Instagram entirely before you touch anything.
The What

Instagram was designed for content. Brands have trained us to think about posting schedules, content pillars, and consistency streaks. But that's not how this system works.

This system treats your Instagram profile exactly like a sales page — because that's what it is. Every element of your profile has a conversion job. Your bio is your headline. Your highlights are your trust builders. Your pinned posts are your social proof section. Your feed is your body copy. And ManyChat is your checkout flow.

The Why

Most people optimize for engagement, follows, and consistency. Those are the wrong metrics. The right metric is conversions — cold strangers clicking through to buy.

When you treat your profile as a sales page, every decision becomes clear. Does this post help convert cold traffic? If yes, it stays. If no, it gets hidden. The profile is always curated, always intentional, always working.

The How

Audit your profile right now with this lens: what job does each element do? Go to your bio. Does each line have a purpose — or is it decorative? Go to your highlights. Do they handle objections in order? Go to your feed. Does every visible post serve a conversion function?

The Filter for Every Decision
Before you post anything, ask: "Does this help a cold stranger decide to buy?" If the answer is yes — post it. If no — skip it or save it for Stories.
Your Task

Write down every element of your current Instagram profile — bio, highlights, pinned posts, top 6 visible feed posts. For each one, write one sentence describing its conversion job. If you can't, that's your gap.

The What

Followers are a vanity metric. Views are a vanity metric. Even likes are a vanity metric when your goal is sales. The only number that matters is conversion rate — what percentage of people who land on your profile take the next step in your funnel.

Rebecca's profile has 13.8K followers and 18 posts. It generated 1.9M views in 30 days and consistently converts cold paid traffic into buyers. That's the system working.

The Why

Here's the math that changes everything: a high-converting profile + paid traffic = sales. You don't need a large following. You need a profile that converts, and a way to drive cold strangers to it.

More followers means more social proof, which helps — but it's not the variable that drives revenue. The profile structure and the ad spend are.

The How

Stop measuring success by follower count or reach. Start measuring it by funnel entries (how many people comment a keyword or DM you) and conversion rate (what percentage of those DMs result in a sale or click-through).

Your ManyChat data will show you this. Your link-in-bio analytics will show you this. Those are the numbers to watch.

More Followers ≠ More Sales
A 10,000-follower account with a high-converting profile will outsell a 100K account with a broken profile structure — every time. The system beats the audience size.
Your Task

Identify your current conversion entry point. Where does someone go after they land on your profile? Is that journey clear? Write down what your current funnel entry point is — or note that you need to create one.

The What

The system has four layers, each with one job: hand the visitor to the next layer. Profile photo → bio → highlights → pinned posts → feed → ManyChat. Nothing exists for decoration. Everything exists for conversion.

Cold ad traffic lands on your profile. The profile does the persuasion work. ManyChat closes the sale. That's the full loop.

The Why

When every layer is working, you have a machine. Cold strangers who have never heard of you become buyers — while you sleep, work, parent, or do whatever you actually want to be doing. That's the promise of this system.

When a layer breaks down — a bio that doesn't qualify, highlights in the wrong order, pinned posts that confuse instead of convert — the machine leaks. Revenue falls off at that point. This is why each layer gets its own module.

The How

Walk through your own profile as if you are a cold stranger who just saw an ad. You don't know who Rebecca is. You don't know the product. You landed on this profile because a video stopped your scroll. What happens next?

Do This Right Now
Open your Instagram profile in incognito mode or ask someone who doesn't follow you to visit it. Watch what they do. Where do they stop? Where do they leave? That's your gap analysis.
Your Task

Map your current conversion path on paper. Profile photo → bio → highlights → pinned posts → feed → where does the stranger end up? Is there a clear funnel entry? Identify the first place the journey breaks down.

Module 2 of 4
Build Your Sales Page
02
Module 2 — 4 Lessons
Setting Up Your Profile to Convert Cold Traffic
Walk away with a fully optimized profile — photo, bio, highlights, and pinned posts.
The What

Your profile photo is the first micro-decision a cold stranger makes about you. Before they read a single word. Before they tap your bio. Before they see your highlights. The photo either earns the next second of their attention or loses it.

The formula is simple: warm and approachable over polished and professional. Real over produced. You look like them, not above them.

The Why

Cold traffic converts when people feel seen. The profile photo's job is to signal "I'm like you" before a word is read. If you look like a stock photo or a brand logo, you've already created distance. If you look like a real person who could be their neighbor — you've earned the next click.

Trust starts with the face. This is true in every sales context, online and off.

The How

Choose a photo where you are smiling, making eye contact with the camera, and the background is simple. Natural light over studio lighting. Approachable expression over "executive headshot" energy.

The photo should match the vibe of your content — if you're a mom building a business from home, your photo should feel like that. If you're a fitness creator, it should feel like that. Consistency between the photo and the content builds trust fast.

Your Task

Update your profile photo if it doesn't pass the "warm and approachable" test. If you don't have a better option yet, note it as a priority to shoot — natural light, smiling, eye contact, simple background.

The What

Your bio has four lines. Each line has one job. Nothing is decorative. This is the most important copy on your entire profile — it's the first thing a cold stranger reads after they've decided your photo is worth a second look.

LineJobRebecca's Bio
Line 1Value prop — the dream they wantHelping everyday women turn ideas into income
Line 2Identity qualifier — "I'm one of you"Mom of 3
Line 3Proof — why should I listen to you?Made over $2M with digital products
Line 4One CTA — link directly to your offer (not a links page)Click the link to see how 👇
The Why

Each line earns the next. Line 1 hooks them with what they want. Line 2 tells them you're one of them — not an authority on a pedestal, but a peer who figured something out. Line 3 gives them a reason to trust you. Line 4 tells them what to do with that trust.

Skip any line and the bio breaks down. Add anything decorative and you dilute the conversion power of every line around it.

The How

Write your bio from scratch using the four-job framework. Don't start with what sounds good — start with what each line's job is. Then write the simplest, clearest version of that job for your specific audience and offer.

Link directly to your offer
Your link-in-bio should go straight to your offer page — not a Linktree, not a links page, not your homepage. Every extra click you add between the CTA and the checkout is a conversion you lose. Cold traffic that is ready to act needs one clear destination. Give them one.

Common Mistakes
Line 3 (proof) trips people up. It doesn't have to be a revenue number — proof can be: students helped, time in the industry, a specific transformation, or a credential that matters to your audience. The goal is "why should I listen to you" answered simply and specifically.

Every line should be a complete, standalone statement. No emoji-heavy fluff. No vague claims. One clear fact per line.

Your Task

Write your four-line bio using the framework. Line 1: value prop. Line 2: identity qualifier. Line 3: your proof. Line 4: one CTA with your link. Read it as a cold stranger. Does each line earn the next?

The What

Highlights are where objections go to die before they ever make it to a DM. They handle the sales conversation passively — no back-and-forth required. A cold stranger can tap through your highlights and resolve every hesitation they have without you being there at all.

Order matters. Skepticism gets neutralized before proof is presented — not the other way around.

🚫
Identity / Anti-Guru
Kill the #1 objection in your niche upfront. ("NOT a guru" — she's one of us.)
❓
Q&A
Answer the hesitations so you don't have to 1:1. The FAQs that stop people from buying.
⭐
Student Wins
Prove it works for people like them — social proof from peers, not polished testimonials.
🎁
Freebie / Start Here
Low-barrier funnel entry. Gives value immediately. Gets them into your world.
The Why

The order of your highlights follows the buyer psychology sequence. A cold stranger's first reaction is skepticism — they're protecting themselves from being sold to. The "NOT a guru" or identity highlight addresses that immediately. Once skepticism is handled, they're open to the Q&A. Once hesitations are resolved, social proof lands harder. Once they trust you, the freebie is an easy yes.

Flip the order — lead with student wins before you've handled the "is this person legit?" question — and the proof doesn't land as hard.

The How

Build four highlights in this order: anti-guru/identity first, Q&A second, student wins third, freebie/start here fourth. Each highlight should have 3–7 stories inside — enough to tell a story, not so many it becomes a scroll obligation.

Your Q&A highlight should answer the top 5–7 objections in your niche. Not the easy ones — the real ones. "I don't have time." "I've tried this before." "I'm not tech-savvy enough." Address them directly and honestly.

Your Task

List the top 5 objections your ideal buyer has before purchasing. These go in your Q&A highlight. Then plan what 3–5 stories would go in each of your four highlights. You don't have to film everything today — just map what each highlight needs to say.

The What

Your three pinned posts take a cold visitor from curious to ready to act. Each pin handles a different part of the buyer's internal conversation. Together, they create a sequence:

I'm like you → Here's what to do → Here's what your life could look like

Pin 1
Relatable Proof
Specific numbers + relatable identity + objection handled in one hook. Makes the outcome feel real and achievable.
"I'm just a regular mom of 3. Last week I made $12,734 from home. I didn't sell anything on Etsy."
Pin 2
The Action Trigger
Highest-converting funnel entry point. Educational carousel format. Delivers real value — and every post (including this one) uses a ManyChat keyword trigger in the CTA.
"Here's EXACTLY what I'd do to make $300–500 ASAP." → Comment keyword → ManyChat DMs the resource
Pin 3
Emotional Proof
Sells the lifestyle, not just the money. Connects the outcome to something bigger than a number.
"My Online Business Changed Our Lives" — 75.5K views
The Why

These three posts handle the complete buyer psychology sequence. First: they need to believe it's possible (Pin 1 — relatable proof). Second: they need a clear next step (Pin 2 — action trigger). Third: they need to feel what the outcome means for their life, not just their bank account (Pin 3 — emotional proof).

The action trigger is your most important post. It's pinned in the center because it gets the most visibility. Every ManyChat funnel entry flows through here.

The How

Create one post for each pin. Pin 1 needs a specific number, a specific identity, and at least one handled objection in the hook. Pin 2 needs a clear ManyChat keyword and an irresistible specific promise. Pin 3 can be more emotional and lifestyle-focused — show what life looks like with the outcome, not just the outcome itself.

On Pin 2 (The Action Trigger)
This is your ManyChat post. The hook promises something specific and immediately actionable. A viewer comments a keyword. ManyChat sends them a DM with the resource — which leads to your offer. This is where most of the revenue comes from.
Your Task

Script your three pinned posts. Start with Pin 2 (the action trigger) — what specific thing could you promise that a cold stranger would immediately want? What would your ManyChat keyword be? Then script Pin 1 (relatable proof with your real numbers) and Pin 3 (the lifestyle/emotional angle).

Module 3 of 4
Build Your Feed
03
Module 3 — 4 Lessons
The 4 Posts Every Feed Needs
Create your foundation feed with intention — every post has a job.
Before You Build
The Post Format Formula

Every feed post needs three things, in this order:

1
Hook
First frame text overlay that stops the scroll. Name a specific person, problem, or number. Clear over clever.
→
2
Body
Delivers on the hook's promise. The structure depends on your post type (see lessons below).
→
3
Trigger
CTA that activates the funnel. Comment a word, DM me, click link. Always one specific action.
Hook Writing Rules
Name a specific person, problem, or number. Create a gap between what they know and what they want to know. Never be clever when you can be clear.
Feed Ratio
What's live on your profile at any given time

You don't post randomly. You curate intentionally toward this ratio:

🔨 Objection Crusher
30%
💰 Proof Post
25%
✨ Curiosity Gap
25%
🎯 Qualifier
20%
Important
This is the ratio of what's visible at any time — not a posting schedule. You hide posts that aren't converting. You feature posts that are. The feed is always curated toward this ratio.
The What

The Qualifier post's job is to call in the exact right person and repel everyone else. It uses hyper-specific identity language so the right viewer stops mid-scroll thinking "that's literally me."

Live Example from Rebecca's Feed
"This is for the moms Googling 'Ways To Make Money From Home' at midnight… Here's what changed our lives." — 30.5K views. The specificity does all the work. "Midnight" is the detail that makes someone feel seen.
The Why

Counter-intuitive truth: the narrower your hook, the wider your real reach. When you say "this is for the moms Googling ways to make money from home at midnight," every single mom who has done that exact thing feels personally addressed. They stop. The people it's not for keep scrolling. Both outcomes are the point — you're qualifying, not casting a net.

Vague hooks get vague results. Specific hooks get buyers.

The How

Your hook for a Qualifier post should name an identity + a specific situation. The body validates that situation and invites them into a better one. The trigger gives them the next step.

Structure: Identity → situation → invitation. "This is for [identity] who [specific situation]. [Validation]. [Here's what to do next]."

Specificity Levels
Too vague: "This is for women who want to make money online." Good: "This is for the moms Googling ways to make money at midnight." Great: "This is for the moms who've tried Etsy, failed, and now wonder if digital products actually work for people like them."
Your Task

Write three Qualifier hooks for your niche. Each one should name a specific identity and a hyper-specific situation. The best one becomes your first Qualifier post. Use the Hook Bank for inspiration — Qualifier category has 63 fill-in-the-blank templates.

The What

The Objection Crusher leads with the objection as the hook — not the answer. The objection in the first frame stops the person who has that exact objection. Then the post dismantles it.

Live Example from Rebecca's Feed
"Think you need a big following to make real money online?" — 35.3K views. The objection is the hook. Everyone who has thought this exact thing stops scrolling. The video then dismantles it with proof and a reframe.
The Why

This post type makes up 30% of the feed — the largest ratio — because objections are the primary reason people don't buy. Every uncrushed objection is a sale that didn't happen. The Objection Crusher post handles them at scale, passively, with every viewer.

These posts are the most reusable posts you'll make. One good objection crusher can generate sales for months because the objection never goes away — it just keeps getting served to new cold viewers.

The How

Start by listing every reason your ideal buyer says they can't buy or won't work. Each one is an Objection Crusher post. The structure:

Hook = the objection stated as a question or challenge ("Think you need a big following…?"). Body = lead with the objection, then dismantle it with your experience, proof, or reframe. Trigger = CTA that moves them forward.

Your Task

List 5 objections your ideal buyer has. Turn the top 2 into Objection Crusher hooks using the formula: state the objection as a question or provocative statement. The Hook Bank Objection Crusher category has 63 templates to fill in.

The What

The Proof Post makes the outcome feel real and achievable — not aspirational and out of reach. It combines specific numbers with a relatable identity. Never vague, never braggy. The specificity is the proof.

Live Example from Rebecca's Feed
"My first digital product was $10. And it has paid my mortgage more times than I can count." — 19.6K views. No income claim. No big number. The specificity of "$10 product" + "paid my mortgage" is what makes it land. It's real and relatable, not polished and distant.
The Why

Cold traffic doesn't know you. They need evidence that the outcome is achievable for someone like them — not someone impressive. The moment your proof sounds too polished or too big, you create distance. They stop identifying with you.

Relatable proof converts better than impressive proof. "I made $12,734 last week as a regular mom of 3" outperforms "I've generated $2M in revenue" — not because the bigger number isn't real, but because the smaller number feels possible.

The How

Structure: Specific result + relatable identity + how it happened. The "how it happened" part handles the "but how?" that every viewer is thinking. Don't just share the result — connect the result to something they can do too.

Proof doesn't have to be financial. Volume-based proof (number of students, number of downloads) and transformation-based proof (what changed for someone who did this) work just as well.

Your Task

Write 3 proof hooks using specific numbers + your relatable identity. At least one should be about a result that feels small enough to be achievable — not your biggest win, your most relatable one. The Hook Bank Proof category has 62 templates.

The What

The Curiosity Gap post creates incomplete information that demands resolution. It forces the watch all the way through and then triggers the DM — which activates ManyChat and closes the sale.

Live Example from Rebecca's Feed
"The PROBLEM with TikTok Shop for Creators… Problem is you are selling" — 19.6K views. The hook teases a revelation. It withholds the conclusion. The viewer watches to find out. At the end: "Comment [keyword] and I'll send you [specific thing]." ManyChat fires. The funnel opens.
The Why

This is your funnel entry post. It has a job the other post types don't: convert a viewer into a DM conversation, and let ManyChat take it from there. Every Curiosity Gap post should have a ManyChat keyword embedded in the CTA.

The curiosity gap is powerful because it promises an answer the viewer wants but can only get by watching, then acting. It rewards attention with a specific resource — which is what ManyChat delivers.

The How

Structure: Tease the insight → withhold the resolution → trigger the DM. The hook promises something they want to know. The body builds toward it without fully delivering it. The trigger invites them to DM you or comment a keyword to get the complete answer.

ManyChat Connection
Every post ends with: "Comment [KEYWORD] and I'll send you [specific thing]." ManyChat automatically DMs everyone who comments that keyword with the resource. The resource leads to your offer. This is the automation loop — and it lives on every post type, not just Curiosity Gap.
Your Task

Identify the one thing in your niche that people most want to know but aren't getting straight answers about. That's your first Curiosity Gap hook. Write it as a problem statement or a teased revelation. Decide what your ManyChat keyword will be and what resource it delivers. The Hook Bank Curiosity Gap category has 62 templates.

Rebecca's Feed — Annotated
Every post has a job. Here's how to read the feed.
Post Key
1
💰 Proof
2
🔨 Objection Crusher
3
✨ Curiosity Gap
4
🔨 Objection Crusher
5
🔨 Objection Crusher
6
💰 Proof
7
🎯 Qualifier
8
💰 Proof
9
✨ Curiosity Gap
10
🎯 Qualifier
11
✨ Curiosity Gap
12
💰 Proof
Post-by-Post Breakdown
1
💰 Proof
“Try THIS to make an extra $100 every day as a mom (That's $3k/month!)”
Proof post with a specific daily number anchored to a monthly translation. '$100/day' feels achievable. '($3k/month!)' reframes it as significant recurring income. The identity marker 'as a mom' qualifies exactly who this is for.
2
🔨 Objection Crusher
“I tried Etsy once… and I made $0.”
Leads with the failure as the hook — the objection itself. Every person who tried Etsy and failed stops scrolling. Vulnerability signals honesty, which builds trust faster than any polished claim. The post then reframes what actually works.
3
✨ Curiosity Gap
“THIS side hustle turned into over $2 MILLION (as a SAHM mom of 3)”
'THIS side hustle' withholds what it is — you have to watch to find out. The identity qualifier '(as a SAHM mom of 3)' immediately makes the result feel attainable for the right viewer. ManyChat keyword at the end captures the warm viewer.
4
🔨 Objection Crusher
“I have nothing new that I could create that isn't already out there”
The objection IS the hook — stated verbatim the way the viewer thinks it. Seeing your own doubt on screen is a pattern interrupt. 27K views confirms this objection is nearly universal in the niche.
5
🔨 Objection Crusher
“Think you need a big following to make real money online?”
The most common objection in the digital products niche, framed as a challenge. 35.3K views — the highest on the feed — confirms it's the objection the target audience holds most. The question format guarantees the skeptic stops to hear the answer.
6
💰 Proof
“Here's the BEST side-hustle to do from home… and it's not what most people think”
Proof post with a curiosity hook that stops anyone who's searched for a side hustle — which is most of the target audience. The 'not what most people think' frame creates a gap they need to close. The video then reveals digital products as the answer and backs it with real proof.
7
🎯 Qualifier
“This is for the moms Googling 'Ways To Make Money From Home' at midnight…”
'Googling at midnight' is the hyper-specific detail that makes the right viewer feel personally called out. It's not 'moms who want to make money' — it's the specific behavior (midnight Googling) that signals both desperation and readiness to act. 30.5K views.
8
💰 Proof
“My first digital product was $10. And it has paid my mortgage more times than I can count.”
'$10 product' removes the intimidation of starting. 'Paid my mortgage' is a tangible, emotionally resonant outcome — not a vague income claim. The specificity of the starting point makes the result feel achievable for a complete beginner.
9
✨ Curiosity Gap
“The PROBLEM with TikTok Shop for Creators… Problem is you are selling”
Names a popular platform to stop the scroll of anyone who's considered it. 'Problem is you are selling' is incomplete by design — viewer watches to find out the alternative. ManyChat keyword fires at the end to capture that warm viewer.
10
🎯 Qualifier
“Digital Product Ideas FOR MOMS”
Simple, direct qualifier using b-roll. The identity label 'FOR MOMS' does all the qualifying work. Works as an entry point for viewers earlier in the awareness journey — not yet ready for a specific offer, but interested in the category.
11
✨ Curiosity Gap
“The BEST WAY to make money online… online is to sell your own”
Teases the mechanism ('sell your own…') without completing the sentence. The text overlay cuts off intentionally — you have to watch to get the full answer. Especially effective with cold ad traffic where thumb-stopping is the only metric that matters in the first frame.
12
💰 Proof
“Here's EXACTLY what I would do if I needed to make extra 💰 from home”
'EXACTLY' signals specificity and promises a complete answer, not general advice. Functions as a soft curiosity gap — viewers watch to get the specific steps. ManyChat keyword at the end converts the warm viewer into a funnel entry.
Module 4 of 4
Turn It On
04
Module 4 — 2 Lessons
Driving Traffic to Your Sales Page
Understand how ads + ManyChat complete the system and turn it on.
The What

ManyChat is an Instagram automation tool that watches your posts for specific comment keywords and automatically sends a DM to anyone who comments that keyword. You set it up once. It runs forever.

Here's the loop: Viewer watches your Curiosity Gap post → you tell them to "comment [KEYWORD] for [resource]" → they comment → ManyChat instantly DMs them the resource → the resource links to your offer → sale happens.

The Why

This is the automation layer that makes the whole system passive. Without ManyChat, you're manually responding to every DM and every comment. With ManyChat, cold strangers who comment on your posts at 2am get an instant, personalized response while you sleep.

ManyChat also signals to the Instagram algorithm that your post is generating engagement — which increases reach, which brings more cold traffic, which generates more comments, which generates more DMs. It compounds.

The How

Set up a ManyChat account (manychat.com) and connect it to your Instagram. Create a keyword automation: when someone comments [your keyword] on [your post], send them a DM with [your resource link].

Your Curiosity Gap post CTA becomes: "Comment [KEYWORD] below and I'll send you [specific thing] in your DMs." Keep the keyword simple — one word, easy to remember.

Keyword Strategy
Use a different keyword per post or per offer so you can track which posts are generating the most DMs. Common formats: "GUIDE", "FREE", "STEPS", or something specific to the content — "AUDIT", "TEMPLATE", "PLAYBOOK". Keep them short.

The resource your ManyChat delivers should be either a freebie that leads to your offer or a direct link to your offer. The goal is a click, then a purchase.

Your Task

Create your first ManyChat automation. Choose one Curiosity Gap post (or your Pin 2 action trigger). Set the keyword. Set the DM it sends. Set where that DM leads. Test it yourself by commenting the keyword from another account.

The What

Ads are the traffic layer. They take your best-performing content — the posts already getting organic engagement — and push them in front of cold audiences who have never heard of you. The profile does the converting. The ads do the reaching.

Two types of ads run in this system: traffic ads and engagement ads. Both can start at $5/day.

The Why

Organic reach is unpredictable and slow. Ads are predictable and scalable. Once your profile is optimized — once the bio, highlights, pins, and feed are all doing their job — adding $5/day in ad spend sends a steady stream of cold traffic into a machine that converts.

You don't run ads to a broken profile. You run ads after the system is built. That's why ads are Module 4, not Module 1.

The How

Start with your best-performing organic content — the post with the highest view count, the most comments, the most DMs. That's your first ad creative. It already works organically. Boost it with $5/day and let it run for 7 days.

Traffic Ads vs. Engagement Ads
Traffic ads drive people to your profile or link. Engagement ads push your posts in front of more people to generate more comments (which then trigger your ManyChat keywords). Run both. Start with $5/day each.

Let the data tell you what to scale. After 7 days, the post with the most ManyChat triggers and the lowest cost-per-DM gets more budget. The others get paused or replaced.

Your Task

Identify your #1 best-performing post by views or engagement. This is your first ad. Set up a $5/day engagement ad in Instagram's Boost Post feature (simple) or Meta Ads Manager (more control). Run it for 7 days. Watch the ManyChat DM volume. That's your feedback loop.

Implementation
Your Launch Sequence
Build in this exact order. Each step sets up the next one.
1
Optimize the Bio
Four lines, four jobs. Value prop → identity qualifier → proof → one CTA with your link. Nothing decorative. Do this first because it's the fastest win and every visitor sees it.
2
Set Up Highlights
Objection/anti-guru first, Q&A second, student wins third, freebie fourth. Order matters. Even rough highlights in the right order beat polished ones in the wrong order.
3
Create the 3 Pinned Posts
Relatable Proof → Action Trigger (with ManyChat keyword) → Emotional Proof. Pin 2 is your priority. It's where your funnel lives. Film and post Pin 2 before anything else.
4
Build the Feed Foundation
One of each post type — minimum 4 posts live. One Qualifier, one Objection Crusher, one Proof Post, one Curiosity Gap. Hide anything that doesn't fit the framework.
5
Set Up ManyChat
Create the keyword automation for your Pin 2 and any Curiosity Gap posts. Test it yourself. The funnel doesn't exist until ManyChat is live.
6
Run Ads
Only after steps 1–5 are done. Start with your best-performing organic post. $5/day traffic + $5/day engagement. Let it run 7 days. Watch the ManyChat volume. Scale what converts.
Your Beta Bonus Resource
The 250-Hook Bank
250 fill-in-the-blank hook templates across all four post types. Every hook you learned in this dashboard — there are 62–63 templates waiting for you in each category. Fill in the brackets and you've got your next post.
